data.title: Compal Electronics Warns AI-Driven Memory Costs May Pressure PC Margins Through 2027
data.description:
Taiwan-based Compal Electronics warned that rising memory prices will continue to weigh on the tech industry through 2027. The company is one of the world's largest makers of notebooks and personal computers for major global brands.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At a press event in Taipei, Compal said global PC and notebook shipments are likely to fall by a low single-digit percentage in 2026. Even so, the company expects its own PC business to stay flat or see mild growth due to its customer mix.

Chief Executive Officer Anthony Peter Bonadero said the pressure comes from memory costs that are rising faster than expected. "It is a true super cycle that we have not really seen," Bonadero said, referring to memory chips.

AI Demand Is Reshaping the Cost Structure

The warning adds fresh detail to a trend already visible across the market, with reports that memory prices have jumped about 50% as AI data centers absorb supply.

According to Compal, memory used to make up about 15% to 18% of a PC's material cost. That share can now reach 35% to 40%, which leaves far less room for profit.

At the same time, the world's three largest memory chip makers are focused on AI servers. Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), SK Hynix, and Micron Technology (MU) have all said demand tied to AI remains very strong.

Bonadero said these suppliers are giving priority to high-bandwidth memory used in AI systems. As a result, less supply is available for everyday devices like PCs and laptops.

What It Means for Investors

As memory costs stay high, PC makers face limited options. They can accept lower margins or pass higher costs on to buyers. Meanwhile, memory suppliers continue to benefit from firm pricing and strong demand tied to AI spending. This supports the broader view that AI growth is changing not only data centers, but also the cost base of consumer hardware.

Taken together, Compal's comments reinforce a simple point. AI-driven memory demand is no longer a short-term issue, and its impact is likely to last well beyond 2026.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Memory Prices Jump 50% as AI Data Centers Tighten Chip Supply
data.description:
Global demand for phones, personal computers, and game consoles is expected to fall this year as rising memory chip costs force companies to raise prices. The main driver of the memory shortage is heavy spending by major U.S. tech firms on AI data centers. As a result, a large share of the world's memory chip supply is being pulled away from consumer devices.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Companies such as OpenAI, Alphabet (GOOGL), and Microsoft (MSFT) are buying huge volumes of memory for AI servers. This has pushed chip makers to focus on higher-margin data center sales. In turn, prices for memory used in everyday electronics have surged.

Samsung Electronics (SSNLF), SK Hynix, and Micron Technology (MU), the three largest memory chip producers, have all reported strong earnings as prices climbed. However, those higher costs are now spreading across consumer markets. Research firms IDC and Counterpoint now expect global smartphone sales to fall by at least 2% this year. That would be the first yearly decline since 2023.

At the same time, IDC expects the global PC market to shrink by about 4.9% in 2026 after solid growth last year. Console sales are also expected to drop by more than 4%, according to TrendForce.

Consumer Devices Face Pricing Pressure

As memory costs rise, hardware makers face difficult choices. They can absorb the higher costs and accept lower margins, or they can raise prices and risk weaker demand. Many firms are already passing some of those costs to buyers.

"Manufacturers might absorb some costs, but given the scale of the shortage, it is certainly going to show up as higher prices for consumers," said Jacob Bourne, an analyst at Emarketer. He added that this trend is likely to weigh on device sales through 2026.

Counterpoint estimates memory prices could rise another 40% to 50% in the first quarter, following a 50% jump last year. Tobey Gonnerman, president of Fusion Worldwide, said, "Over the last two quarters, we've seen 1,000% price inflation in some products and pricing is continuing to rise."

The impact is expected to hit low and mid-range device makers the hardest. These include Xiaomi (XIACF), Lenovo (LNVGY), and TCL Technology, which operate with thinner margins. TrendForce said Dell Technologies (DELL)  and Lenovo were planning price increases of up to 20% early in 2026

Meanwhile, Apple (AAPL) is seen as better positioned than most rivals. The company has strong pricing power and long-term supply contracts that limit exposure to sudden price swings. Apple also tends to keep U.S. iPhone prices steady between product launches.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: M&A News: EQT Stock Rises on $3.7B Coller Capital Acquisition, Expands into Secondaries Market
data.description:
EQT AB (SE:EQT) shares were up more than 4% at the time of writing, after the Swedish buyout group announced an agreement to acquire U.K.-based private equity firm Coller Capital for $3.7 billion. EQT's CEO Per Franzén stated that Coller's acquisition will mark its entry into the secondaries market, "a natural and important step in EQT's strategic development." The acquisition is subject to regulatory approvals and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EQT Seeks Growth in Secondaries Space with Coller Deal

Coller Capital is a global secondaries firm with nearly $50 billion in total assets under management (AUM). EQT's acquisition of Coller includes a base consideration of $3.2 billion, paid via EQT shares issued at Kr355 per share, and up to $500 million in cash payments contingent on certain performance milestones.

EQT believes that the deal would boost the prospects of both companies, enhancing their position in the secondaries market, which is expected to more than double by 2030. The company expects the deal to increase the relevance of its platform for both institutional and private wealth clients. EQT noted that the secondary market grew 41% in 2025, with deal volumes reaching $226 billion.

Franzén expressed confidence in EQT's ability to double the size of Coller's business in less than four years. As part of the deal, Coller Capital's minority shareholder, State Street, will become EQT's shareholder. 

Meanwhile, EQT announced that it ended 2025 with total AUM of €‌​​‌​270 billion, with fee-generating AUM rising to €‌​​‌​141 billion from €‌​​‌​136 billion at the end of December 2024. EQT highlighted that it announced total gross fund exits of €‌​​19 ​‌billion. Also, the company realized €14 billion for its co-investors, making 2025 its "most active exit year ever" based on total realizations. EQT proposed a total dividend of Kr5, compared to Kr4.30 in the previous year.

Is EQT Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, EQT AB stock scores a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on one Buy and one Hold recommendation. The average EQT stock price target of Kr 379.50 indicates that shares are fully priced at current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is IperionX Stock (IPX) Rising?
data.description:
IperionX Limited (IPX), a titanium metals company, is drawing attention after it said it will supply titanium parts for a U.S. Army program, a deal investors see as an early win.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company received a $300,000 prototype order from American Rheinmetall, a defense supplier, to make about 700 lightweight titanium components. These parts will be used in U.S. Army heavy ground combat systems.

While the order itself is not large, it matters because it shows that IperionX's technology is being used in a real military project.

Why the Deal Is Important

IperionX will make the parts in the U.S. using recycled titanium, rather than imported material. The company said its process allows it to produce titanium at lower cost and with lower energy use than traditional methods.

The timing also helps. Titanium has recently been listed as a critical metal by the U.S. government as it looks to reduce reliance on foreign suppliers. IperionX said it is currently the only domestic producer of commercial-scale primary titanium metal in the U.S., giving it a unique position.

The order value is small, but investors are looking ahead. The company said the prototype work could lead to a larger agreement, which is helping lift interest in the stock.

CEO Taso Arima said the order shows that IperionX's recycled titanium can be used on important U.S. military platforms. He added that the company is well-placed to support U.S. defense needs with domestic and secure supply.

Is IPX Stock a Buy?

The stock of IperionX has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among two Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on two unanimous Buy recommendations issued in the last three months. The average IPX price target of $74.00 implies 42.69% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: OpenAI Explores Massive New Funding Round at Up to $830 Billion Valuation
data.description:
OpenAI is in talks to raise more than $50 billion in a new funding round, according to people familiar with the matter. The discussions point to a possible valuation of about $750 billion to $830 billion, which would represent a sharp increase from its prior level. OpenAI was last valued at about $500 billion in the fall of 2025, when some employees were allowed to sell shares in a private transaction.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company, best known for ChatGPT, is not yet profitable and continues to spend heavily as it builds and runs large AI systems. OpenAI has committed to spending more than $1.4 trillion on AI chips, data centers, and talent in the coming years. The company has said these costs reflect "the immense cost of chips, data centers and talent" needed to support wider use of AI.

Middle East Funds Step In as AI Costs Rise

Meanwhile, Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman has been meeting with major investors in the Middle East to support the new funding round. Bloomberg reported that Altman recently spoke with state-backed investment funds in Abu Dhabi during a visit to the region. The talks remain early, and the final amount raised could still change. At the same time, OpenAI has also held talks with Amazon (AMZN) about raising at least $10 billion, as earlier reported. OpenAI declined to comment on the discussions. The Information previously reported that the company had explored raising tens of billions at a $750 billion valuation.

This effort fits into a broader trend across the AI industry, where capital needs are rising quickly. Several leading AI developers, including Anthropic and xAI, have also turned to Middle East investors. Anthropic is reportedly in talks to raise new funding at a valuation of about $350 billion. OpenAI has already raised money from MGX, an Abu Dhabi-based technology fund, and has partnered with G42 to develop a large data center in the United Arab Emirates.

At the same time, competition is intensifying, with rivals such as Alphabet (GOOGL) continuing to invest heavily in AI. Even so, OpenAI and ChatGPT remain among the most widely used AI products worldwide. For investors, the talks underline how fast AI spending is growing and how much capital is now required to compete at the top level.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Claude. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/22/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb after Indexes Rebound, Inflation Data in Sight
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Thursday after the three indexes marked a major rebound in the previous session, aided by President Trump's easing tariff stance. Trump announced at the World Economic Forum that he would not proceed with his planned tariffs on February 1 and mentioned that no force would be used to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.77%, 0.52%, and 0.29%, respectively, at 3:59 a.m. EST on January 22.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, the three major averages closed higher. The Dow added 1.21%, while the S&P 500 gained 1.16%, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.18%. Trump announced he would halt new tariffs on Europe scheduled for February 1. He also revealed a "framework" agreement on Greenland, easing global investor worries over a forced acquisition.

Turning toward the economic calendar, investors now focus on Thursday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge. Data on weekly initial jobless claims will also be released today.

On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Intel (INTC), Visa (V), and GE Aerospace (GE).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.23%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $60.49 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,827 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today following U.S. President Trump's announcement of a "framework" deal for Greenland and his decision to walk back planned tariffs on Europe.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets also rebounded on January 22 amid a cooling geopolitical environment.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.17%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.14%, while the Shenzhen Component added 0.67%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei advanced 1.73%, and the Topix surged 0.74%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: JPMorgan (JPM), Allen & Co. Score Big in Netflix-Paramount Battle for Warner Bros. Discovery
data.description:
In Netflix's (NFLX) and Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) battle to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Allen & Company are emerging as clear winners.  According to the SEC filings, both banks will earn $90 million each in advisory fees, guaranteed regardless of whether Netflix or Paramount Skydance wins the bidding war.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, JPMorgan is not only advising on the deal but also helping finance the restructuring that made the sale possible. The bank has already earned $189 million in financing and related fees for arranging a massive $17.5 billion bridge loan, one of the largest non‑investment‑grade bridge loans ever issued on Wall Street.

After adding all advisory and financing fees, JPMorgan is set to make about $282 million from the Warner Bros. Discovery deal process.

Meanwhile, Allen & Company will earn at least $90 million in advisory fees. It has already collected part of that compensation and will receive the rest when the deal closes.

The Bidding War Intensifies

The bidding war intensified after Netflix recently raised its offer to $83 billion for Warner Bros.' studio and streaming assets, shifting to an all‑cash structure to strengthen its position.

At the same time, Paramount Skydance has a $108 billion hostile tender offer for the entire company, putting pressure on Warner Bros.' shareholders as the deadline approaches.

It is worth noting that Warner Bros. Discovery has spent a large amount on restructuring and advisory fees as it prepares for a sale, splitting its cable news and sports assets (including CNN) from its film and TV operations.

Is JPM a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on JPM stock based on 13 Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. Further, the average JPMorgan price target of $347.35 per share implies 15% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Paramount Skydance Stock (PSKY) Rises as It Eyes WBD Hostile Bid Extension
data.description:
Shares of Paramount Skydance (PSKY) rose modestly on Wednesday amid reports that the company is considering an extension of its January 21 deadline for its hostile bid to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). According to On The Money, CEO David Ellison is not yet raising the $30 per share offer and is not getting bogged down by Netflix's (NFLX) revised all-cash deal.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On January 20, Netflix and WBD changed their deal terms to all-cash, citing a more favorable deal structure. WBD also moved up a shareholder vote on the proposed Netflix deal to February from May, to add pressure on Paramount Skydance.

Ellison Won't Budge Just Yet

Ellison has the backing of his billionaire father, Oracle (ORCL) CEO Larry Ellison and private equity firm RedBird Capital Partners. According to sources, the team plans to announce an extension today, though no specific timeline has been confirmed. This would give WBD shareholders more time to weigh Paramount Skydance's offer against Netflix's. Additionally, they are pressing forward with a lawsuit, alleging that WBD CEO David Zaslav is unfairly favoring Netflix's deal due to his close ties with CEO Ted Sarandos.

The report also highlighted that Ellison has one more ace up his sleeve. Paramount Skydance is reportedly prepared to raise its offer to a maximum of $33 per share for all of WBD, if necessary. This would value the deal at $80 billion, excluding debt. Such a deal could intensify pressure on Netflix, which is offering $27.75 per share in all-cash for WBD's streaming and studio assets.

The Paramount team also met with European and UK regulators, arguing that a Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. combination would be less monopolistic than a Netflix-Warner Bros. pairing. Ellison is also arguing that Netflix's deal would face more stringent anticompetitive scrutiny.

Is PSKY a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analysts remain cautious on Paramount Skydance's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, PSKY stock has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on one Buy, seven Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Paramount Skydance price target of $14.08 implies 21.4% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PSKY shares have surged 10%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Moderna Stock (MRNA) Hits a New 52-Week High — Can the Rally Last Through 2026?
data.description:
Moderna (MRNA) stock surged about 16% on Wednesday and hit a new 52‑week high of $50. The strong rally came after Moderna and Merck (MRK) released five‑year follow‑up results from their melanoma vaccine trial, which showed a 49% reduction in recurrence or death when combined with Keytruda. However, the surge has led investors to wonder whether this marks a comeback or just a short-lived spike in MRNA stock.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's Driving Optimism for 2026?

Moderna's growth outlook for 2026 is better than it has been since the pandemic boom. The company is shifting from a single-product COVID business to a diversified biotech with multiple revenue streams.

Its personalized cancer vaccine with Merck is the biggest long-term driver, with investors watching for Phase 3 progress, expansion into more cancers, and new Keytruda combo data.

The company is also building a steady respiratory‑vaccine business with its flu, RSV, and combo shots, giving it recurring annual revenue beyond COVID. At the same time, Moderna is cutting costs, improving manufacturing efficiency, and moving closer to breakeven.

Moreover, new government contracts, cancer‑vaccine partnerships, and global distribution deals further support and stabilize its growth outlook.

Despite these positive factors, Moderna faces several hurdles. Its revenue is not expected to match its pandemic-era level anytime soon. Also, the company is competing directly with Pfizer (PFE), BioNTech (BNTX), GSK (GSK), and Sanofi (SNY), all of which are investing heavily in respiratory and oncology vaccines.

What Is the Future Price of MRNA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, Moderna stock has a Hold consensus rating based on one Buy, 15 Hold, and four Sell recommendations. The average MRNA stock price target of $29.93 indicates 39.91% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
